summary: DHH announces his trip to O'Reilly's Open Source Conference (OSCON) 2005 in Portland. He mentions the long 17-hour journey from Copenhagen but expresses excitement about meeting fellow Rubyists. He has a packed schedule including a keynote, a session, and a three-hour tutorial on Ruby on Rails.
key_points: DHH is traveling to OSCON 2005 in Portland with a grueling 17-hour journey
He has a full conference schedule: keynote, session, and three-hour tutorial
Rails was gaining significant visibility at major open source conferences by mid-2005
money_quotes: Nothing like the prospect of a grueling 17 hour journey to get you pumped in the morning.
It's going to be a g-oh good time for Ruby on Rails!
key_insight: By mid-2005, Ruby on Rails had achieved enough prominence to earn DHH a keynote slot at one of the most important open source conferences.
